channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Communities launch cleanup after severe weather and tornadoes churn across Midwest
item.description: At least 66 tornado reports were submitted across multiple states on Friday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.content:encoded:
At least 66 tornado reports were submitted across multiple states on Friday, according to the National Weather Service.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Will the DHS shutdown affect security for the World Cup?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the tournament in America in this moment.
item.content:encoded: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the tournament in America in this moment.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: 8 children killed in a shooting in Louisiana, police say
item.description: The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Real estate investors are buying up long-term care facilities. Residents can suffer
item.description: Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.
item.content:encoded:
Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.

(Image credit: Taylor Glascock for KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Jordan Rau

item.title: Iowa went all-in on school choice. It's hurting this city's public schools
item.description: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.
item.content:encoded:
With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.

(Image credit: Cliff Jette for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
item.description: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.
item.content:encoded:
The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Reporting on the policies that impact affordability for Americans
item.description: With affordability a top issue for more and more Americans, NPR's Jennifer Ludden talks about her reporting on the policies that impact access to housing and food.
item.content:encoded: With affordability a top issue for more and more Americans, NPR's Jennifer Ludden talks about her reporting on the policies that impact access to housing and food.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Trump signs order fast tracking review of psychedelics for mental health disorders
item.description: President endorses psilocybin and ibogaine: "Can I have some, please?"
item.content:encoded:
President endorses psilocybin and ibogaine: "Can I have some, please?"

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Challenges facing individuals in America who are eligible for U.S. citizenship
item.description: New data shows last year was one of the most volatile years ever for naturalizations, as immigration policy changes and scrutiny affected people's desire to make the final leap to become an American.
item.content:encoded: New data shows last year was one of the most volatile years ever for naturalizations, as immigration policy changes and scrutiny affected people's desire to make the final leap to become an American.
item.dc:creator: Anusha Mathur

item.title: What's it like to negotiate with Iran? We asked people who have done it
item.description: Experts who spent months negotiating a 2015 nuclear agreement with Iran say mutual mistrust, starkly different negotiating styles, and the complexity of the issues make a quick deal unlikely.
item.content:encoded:
Experts who spent months negotiating a 2015 nuclear agreement with Iran say mutual mistrust, starkly different negotiating styles, and the complexity of the issues make a quick deal unlikely.

(Image credit: Farooq Naeem)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman
